name: launch
description: Launch a Chromium browser and create a session. Default: Google Chrome. Use 'browser' to pick another installed browser. Use 'profile' to launch an isolated browser instance with its own profile.

browser.description: Browser to launch: chrome (default), edge, brave, arc, vivaldi, chromium, or canary

profile.description: Profile name for isolated browser instance. Use 'new' to auto-generate an ID. Default profile is shared and persistent.

name: navigate
description: Navigate to a URL. Auto-dismisses cookie/popup banners. Auto-prints a compact page summary showing URL, title, inputs, buttons, and links.

url.description: URL to navigate to (https:// prefix added if missing)

name: read
description: Extract readable text content from the page, like browser reader mode. Strips navigation, sidebars, ads, and UI chrome. Returns clean text with headings, lists, and paragraphs. Best for articles, docs, and information retrieval.

name: text
description: Get full page content in reading order, interleaving static text with interactive elements (numbered refs). Like a screen reader view. Use ref numbers in click/type/etc. Best for understanding complex pages where you need both content and interaction targets.

name: dom
description: Get compact DOM of the page. Scripts, styles, SVGs, and hidden elements are stripped. Use selector to scope, max_tokens to limit output size.

selector.description: CSS selector to scope DOM extraction

name: axtree
description: Get accessibility tree. Use interactive=true for a flat numbered list of actionable elements (most compact). After running with interactive=true, use ref numbers as selectors in click/type/etc. Use diff=true to show changes since last snapshot.

interactive.description: Show only interactive elements with ref numbers

diff.description: Show only changes since last snapshot

selector.description: CSS selector to scope the tree

name: observe
description: Show interactive elements as ready-to-use commands (e.g., 'click 1', 'type 3 <text>'). Generates ref map as side effect.

name: find
description: Find an element by natural language description (e.g., 'login button', 'search input').

query.description: Description of the element to find

name: screenshot
description: Capture a screenshot. Expensive (~500+ vision tokens). Prefer dom/axtree/read/text for most tasks. Use ref or selector to crop to a specific element. Defaults to 800px wide; use scale to adjust (e.g. scale:0.5 for half size). Use output to save to a file path without returning base64.

ref.description: Ref number from axtree -i or observe. Crops to that element with padding. Most efficient way to screenshot a specific element.

pad.description: Padding in pixels around ref/selector crop (default: 48)

selector.description: CSS selector to capture only that element's bounding box

scale.description: Scale factor (e.g. 0.5 = half size, 2 = double). Default scales to 800px wide. Coordinates map proportionally: page_x = screenshot_x / scale.

full.description: Capture entire scrollable page, not just the viewport

output.description: Save to this path instead of returning the image. Returns text confirmation only, no base64.

name: pdf
description: Save the current page as a PDF file.

name: click
description: Click an element. Waits up to 5s for it to appear, scrolls into view, then clicks. Accepts a CSS selector, coordinates (e.g., '550,197'), ref number from axtree -i, or use --text prefix to find by visible text.

target.description: CSS selector, 'x,y' coordinates, ref number, or '--text Some Text'

name: doubleclick
description: Double-click an element. Same targeting as click.

target.description: CSS selector, 'x,y' coordinates, ref number, or '--text Some Text'

name: rightclick
description: Right-click an element. Same targeting as click.

target.description: CSS selector, 'x,y' coordinates, ref number, or '--text Some Text'

name: hover
description: Hover over an element. Same targeting as click.

target.description: CSS selector, 'x,y' coordinates, ref number, or '--text Some Text'

name: focus
description: Focus an element without clicking it.

name: clear
description: Clear an input field or contenteditable element.

name: type
description: Focus a specific input element and type text into it. Use 'keyboard' instead for typing at the current caret position (rich editors like Slack, Google Docs).

selector.description: CSS selector or ref number of the input element

name: fill
description: Fill multiple form fields in one call. Each entry maps a CSS selector (or ref number) to a value. More efficient than multiple type calls.

fields.description: Map of CSS selector/ref -> value to type. Example: {"#email": "user@example.com", "#password": "secret"}

name: keyboard
description: Type text at the current caret position without changing focus. Essential for rich text editors (Slack, Google Docs, Notion) where 'type' would reset the cursor.

text.description: Text to type at current caret

name: paste
description: Paste text via ClipboardEvent. Works with apps that intercept paste (Google Docs, Notion). Faster than keyboard for large text.

name: press
description: Press a key or key combo. Examples: Enter, Tab, Escape, Ctrl+A, Meta+C, Shift+Enter. On macOS, use Meta (not Ctrl) for app shortcuts.

name: select
description: Select option(s) from a <select> dropdown by value or label text.

selector.description: CSS selector or ref number of the <select> element

name: upload
description: Upload file(s) to a file input element.

selector.description: CSS selector or ref number of the file input

name: drag
description: Drag from one element to another.

from.description: CSS selector or ref number of the source element

to.description: CSS selector or ref number of the target element

name: scroll
description: Scroll the page or an element. Directions: up, down, top, bottom. Can scope to a container element for apps with custom scroll areas.

target.description: Direction (up/down/top/bottom), CSS selector to scroll into view, or selector followed by direction

name: eval
description: Evaluate a JavaScript expression in the page context and return the result.

name: dialog
description: Set a one-shot handler for the next JavaScript dialog (alert/confirm/prompt). Run BEFORE the action that triggers the dialog.

text.description: Text to enter for prompt dialogs

name: waitfor
description: Wait for an element to appear on the page.

name: waitfornav
description: Wait for page navigation to complete (readyState=complete).

name: cookies
description: Manage browser cookies. Actions: get (list all), set (name value [domain]), clear (all), delete (name [domain]).

domain.description: Cookie domain (optional, defaults to current hostname)

name: console
description: View browser console output. Actions: show (recent logs), errors (errors only), listen (stream live).

name: network
description: Capture or show network requests. 'capture [seconds] [filter]' records traffic. 'show [filter]' displays last capture.

name: block
description: Block network requests by resource type or URL pattern. Types: images, css, fonts, media, scripts. Use '--ads' for ad/tracker blocking. Use 'off' to disable.

name: viewport
description: Set viewport size. Presets: mobile (375x667), iphone (390x844), ipad (820x1180), tablet (768x1024), desktop (1280x800). Or specify width and height.

preset_or_width.description: Preset name or width in pixels

height.description: Height in pixels (when using numeric width)

name: zoom
description: Zoom the page in or out. See more content at lower zoom (e.g., 50% shows 2x more) with same token cost. Coordinate clicks auto-adjust for zoom level.

name: frames
description: List all frames and iframes on the page.

name: frame
description: Switch to a frame by ID, name, or CSS selector. Use 'main' to return to the top frame.

target.description: Frame ID, name, CSS selector, or 'main'

name: tabs
description: List all tabs owned by the current session.

name: tab
description: Switch to a session-owned tab by ID.

name: newtab
description: Open a new tab in the current session, optionally navigating to a URL.

url.description: URL to open in the new tab

name: close
description: Close the current tab.

name: back
description: Go back in browser history.

name: forward
description: Go forward in browser history.

name: reload
description: Reload the current page.

name: activate
description: Bring the browser window to the front (macOS). Use when the user needs to see or interact with the browser directly.

name: minimize
description: Minimize the browser window (macOS). Use after the user has finished interacting with the browser directly.

name: humanclick
description: Click with human-like mouse movement (Bezier curve path, variable timing). Helps avoid bot detection.

target.description: CSS selector, 'x,y' coordinates, ref number, or '--text Some Text'

name: humantype
description: Type with human-like variable delays and occasional typo corrections. Helps avoid bot detection.

selector.description: CSS selector or ref number of the input element

name: lock
description: Lock the active tab for exclusive access by this session.

name: unlock
description: Release the tab lock.

name: download
description: Manage downloads. 'path <dir>' sets download directory. 'list' shows downloaded files.

name: search
description: Search the web using a real browser. Navigates to a search engine, submits the query, and extracts results. Default engine: Google. Use engine parameter for alternatives.

engine.description: Search engine: google (default), bing, duckduckgo, or a custom URL (query appended)

name: readurls
description: Read multiple URLs in parallel. Opens each URL in a new tab, extracts readable content from each, and returns combined results. Closes tabs when done.

name: feedback
description: Send feedback about your webact experience. Call once before the conversation ends.

rating.description: Rating 1-5 (1=poor, 5=excellent). Did webact help you complete browser tasks reliably?

comment.description: Focus on improvements only. Which tools failed or behaved unexpectedly (tool names, errors, selectors that didn't match)? What workarounds did you use? What features or fixes would have made the task easier? Do not recap what you did or list what worked.

name: config
description: Get or set webact configuration. Config file: ~/.config/webact/webact.json

action.description: get: show config, set: update a key

key.description: Config key to set (telemetry or feedback)

name: media
description: Emulate media features: dark/light mode, print media, reduced motion. Combine multiple options in one call. Use 'reset' to clear all media emulation.

name: animations
description: Control page animations via playback rate. Pauses/resumes JS-driven animations. For CSS animations, combine with 'media reduce-motion'.

action.description: pause: freeze all animations, resume: restore normal playback, slow: 10% speed

name: security
description: Control browser security settings. Use ignore-certs for staging/dev sites with self-signed certificates.

action.description: ignore-certs: accept self-signed/invalid certificates, strict: restore normal certificate validation

name: storage
description: Inspect and manage localStorage and sessionStorage for the current page origin. Note: storage is origin-wide — clearing affects all tabs/windows on the same origin.

action.description: get: list storage items, set: set a key, remove: delete a key, clear: clear storage

target.description: For clear: which storage to clear. 'everything' clears storage + cache + cookies + service workers.

session.description: If true, operate on sessionStorage instead of localStorage (for set/remove)

name: sw
description: Manage service workers for the current page. Use when a page shows stale content or has offline cache issues. Note: service workers are origin-wide — unregistering affects all tabs/windows on the same origin.

action.description: list: show registered workers, unregister: remove all workers, update: trigger update check

name: kill
description: Kill the current browser session and clean up its profile. Only works on custom profiles (not the default). Terminates the browser process, removes profile directory and state files.

name: batch
description: Execute multiple actions sequentially in one call. Stops on first error. Smart waits: auto-waits 500ms after state-changing actions (navigate, click, fill, select, type). Per-action 'wait' field overrides smart default.

actions.description: Array of actions to execute. Each action has 'tool' (command name) and tool-specific fields (same as individual tool params). Optional 'wait' (ms) per action.

tool.description: Command name (click, type, screenshot, press, navigate, waitfor, etc.)

wait.description: Wait this many ms after this action (overrides smart default)

delay.description: Global delay in ms between every action (additive with smart waits). Default: 0

name: grid
description: Overlay a coordinate grid on the page for targeting elements in canvas/image-heavy apps. Each cell displays its center coordinate. The overlay is transparent and non-interactive.

spec.description: Grid spec: '8x6' (cols x rows), '50' (pixel cell size), 'off' (remove), or omit for default 10x10

name: install
description: Register webact as an MCP server with all detected clients (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Desktop, etc.) without re-downloading the binary. Run after installing a new MCP client.
